IMGT-ONTOLOGY is the first ontology for immunogenetics and immunoinformatics and the global reference in the domain. It was created as early as in 1989 to manage the complexity of immunogenetics data in IMGT®, the international ImMunoGeneTics information system® (https://www.imgt.org). IMGT-ONTOLOGY provides a semantic specification of the terms to be used in immunogenetics and immunoinformatics and manages the related knowledge, thus allowing the standardization for immunogenetics data from genome, proteome, genetics, two-dimensional (2D) and three-dimensional (3D) structures.

Cifurtilimab is derived from dacetuzumab and targets and binds to CD40, a stimulatory immunoreceptor expressed on antigen-presenting cells (APCs) and certain types of tumor cells. Once bound to CD40, cifurtilimab promotes CD40 signaling. This activation results in activation and expansion of the target cells (i.e. B cells, dendritic cells (DCs), and macrophages) which activate IG class switching in B cells and enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Cifurtilimab is an IgG1-Kappa afucosylated antibody resulting in increased of FcyR binding ability, to enhance antibody-dependent cytoxicity (ADCC) or complement-dependent cytoxicity (CDC) against tumor cells.

Toripalimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, toripalimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Toripalimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Favezelimab targets and binds to lymphocyte activation gene 3 (LAG3, CD223), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to LAG3, favezelimab blocks its binding to its ligands expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APC). This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by the LAG3 signaling resulting in activation and proliferation of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response. Favezelimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

MOA: immunological agent for active immunization (antineoplastic), cancer vaccine. baloramotide 10983 (PL120, RL82) and cadalimogene ixalentivec 10984 (PL120, RL82) compose CMB305, a prime-boost immuno-oncology agent, with baloramotide being used as "boost". cadalimogene ixalentivec (10984, LV305, LV-305) is a hybrid vector from the ZVex(TM) discovery platform that specifically targets dendritic cells in vivo and delivers the RNA for NY-ESO-1, enabling the dendritic cells to express the entire tumor antigen and potentially induce a diverse set of CTLs targeting NY-ESO-1 in tumors. baloramotide (10983, G305, G-305) is designed to boost the CTL response via the induction of antigen-specific CD4 helper T cells. It consists of recombinant NY-ESO-1 protein formulated with the Toll-like receptor 4 (TLR4) agonist glucopyranosyl lipid A (GLA) adjuvant-stable emulsion (GLA-SE).

GlymaxX technology (defucosylation) : Clonal CHO cell line, coexpressing the RMD enzyme (GDP-6-deoxy-D-lyxo-4-hexulose reductase). The RMD enzyme converts GDP-4-keto-6-deoxy-D-mannose into GDP-rhamnose (a sugar which cannot be metabolized by the cell) thereby deflecting a precursor for the de novo synthesis of GDP-fucose. As a result the RMD co-expressing CHO cells secrete the antibody that is almost completely defucosylated (≥ 90 %). Budigalimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, budigalimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. Tocilizumab targets and binds to interleukin 6 receptor (IL6R), a protein complex consisting of this protein and interleukin 6 signal transducer (IL6GT/gp130) a cytokine responsible for participating in the processes of inflammation and the maturation of B cells. Once bound to IL6R, tocilizumab effectively obstructs IL6-IL6R signaling, encompassing both the classic and trans IL6 signaling pathways through the Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitors. This interference leads to the prevention of both anti-inflammatory (phosphoinositide-3-kinase (PI3K) and protein kinase B / PKB pathway) and pro-inflammatory responses (signal transducer and activator of transcription 3-STAT3 and MAPK (mitogen-activated protein kinase) pathway). Tocilizumab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and induce antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC).

Cemiplimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, cemiplimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) expressed on tumor cells, and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2), primarily expressed on antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Cemiplimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Sugemalimab targets and binds to programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274,PD-L1), an immunosuppressive ligand expressed on tumor cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to CD274, sugemalimab blocks its binding to its receptor programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1) expressed on activated T cells, leaving the PDCD1 pathway intact through its second ligand, PDCD1LG2 (PD-L2), which is less expressed in tumor cells, to maintain inhibitory signals mediated by PDCD1LG2/PDCD1. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Sugemalimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Siltuximab targets and binds to interleukin 6 (IL6), a cytokine responsible for participating in the processes of inflammation and the maturation of B cells. Once bound to IL6, siltuximab effectively obstructs IL6-IL6R signaling, encompassing both the classic and trans IL6 signaling pathways through the Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitors. This interference leads to the prevention of both anti-inflammatory (phosphoinositide-3-kinase (PI3K) and protein kinase B / PKB pathway) and pro-inflammatory responses (signal transducer and activator of transcription 3-STAT3 and MAPK (mitogen-activated protein kinase) pathway). Siltuximab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and induce antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC).

Cetrelimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, cetrelimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Cetrelimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

The IDENTIFICATION axiom of the Formal IMGT-ONTOLOGY or IMGT-Kaleidoscope postulates that molecules, cells, tissues, organs, organisms or populations, their processes and relations, have to be identified. The IDENTIFICATION axiom has generated the concepts of identification which provide the terms and rules to identify an entity, its processes and its relations. In molecular biology, the concepts of identification allow to identify the molecular components, the molecules, their processes and their relations at the genome, transcriptome, proteome, structure and function levels.

Inebilizumab targets and binds to CD19 antigen, a member of the immunoglobulin superfamily (IgSF). CD19 is continuously and stably expressed on all stages of B lineage differentiation on the surface of B cell lymphocytes. Once bound to CD19, inebilizumab depletes circulating CD19+ B cells, thus suppressing inflammatory responses and impairing B-cell-dependent T-cell activation. Inebilizumab is an IgG1-kappa afucosylated antibody with increased ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and induce antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC) and complement-dependent cytotoxicity (CDC) for enhanced efficacy against plasma cells and auto-antibodies production. Phase describes the level of a trial required of drugs before (and after) they are routinely used in clinical practice: - Phase I trials assess toxic effects on humans (not many people participate in them, and usually without controls); - Phase ll trials assess therapeutic benefit (usually involving a few hundred people, usually with controls, but not always); - Phase III trials compare the new treatment against standard (or placebo) treatment (usually a full randomised controlled trial). At this point, a drug can be approved for community use. - Phase IV monitors a new treatment in the community, often to evaluate longterm safety and effectiveness. [Glossary of Terms in The Cochrane Collaboration] A trial can be of a combination phase (e.g., I/II). The concept of phase is not applicable to trials studying certain interventions (e.g., device, procedure, behavioral)

Relatlimab targets and binds to lymphocyte activation gene 3 (LAG3, CD223), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to LAG3, relatlimab blocks its binding to its ligands expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APC). This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by the LAG3 signaling resulting in activation and proliferation of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response. Relatlimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

pegdinetanib is an adnectin which represents a new format, alternative to monoclonal antibody, by using the fibronectin domain F10 as a scaffold, developed by Adnexus. Pegylation site: Cys C93. Doubly methoxy-PEGylated maleimide derivative, with a molecular mass of 40 kDaI. Adnexus is a Bristol-Myers Squibb R&D Company, located at the BMS Waltham facility, Massachusetts U.S.A. It focused on the discovery and development of a new class of targeted biologics called Adnectins™. Adnexus was formed in 2002 and was acquired by Bristol-Myers Squibb Company (BMS) in 2007. Mode of action. Adnectins are small proteins (10 to 12 kDa) that are derived from the 10th type III domain of human fibronectin (10Fn3). Adnectins share with antibody variable domains a beta-sheet sandwich fold with diversified loops, but differ from antibodies in primary sequence and have a simpler, single-domain structure without disulfide bonds. As a consequence, Adnectins bind targets with affinity and specificity as high as those of antibodies, but are easier to manipulate genetically and compatible with bacterial expression systems. Socazolimab targets and binds to programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274,PD-L1), an immunosuppressive ligand expressed on tumor cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to CD274, socazolimab blocks its binding to its receptor programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1) expressed on activated T cells, leaving the PDCD1 pathway intact through its second ligand, PDCD1LG2 (PD-L2), which is less expressed in tumor cells, to maintain inhibitory signals mediated by PDCD1LG2/PDCD1. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Socazolimab is an IgG1-lambda antibody with ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and induce antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity (ADCC) for potential enhanced efficacy in certain tumor types.

It comprises six leafconcepts. 'conventional_with_leader' and 'conventional_without_leader' identify any (coding or not coding) gene, with or without leader L region (or signal peptide), respectively, other than the immunoglobulin (IG) or T cell receptor (TR) genes of the vertebrate adaptive immune response. The other four leafconcepts refer to the IG and TR genes and are specific to immunogenetics: three of these leafconcepts, 'variable' (V), 'diversity' (D) and 'joining' (J) identify the IG and TR genes that rearrange at the DNA level in the B and T cells and code the V, D and J regions, respectively, of the IG and TR variable domains; the fourth leafconcept, 'constant' (C), identifies the IG  and TR genes that code the C region of the IG and TR chains.

Geptanolimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, geptanolimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Geptanolimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Selicrelumab targets and binds to CD40, a stimulatory immunoreceptor expressed on antigen-presenting cells (APCs) and certain types of tumor cells. Once bound to CD40, selicrelumab promotes CD40 signaling. This activation results in activation and expansion of the target cells (i.e. B cells, dendritic cells (DCs), and macrophages) which activate IG class switching in B cells and enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Selicrelumab is an IgG2-Kappa antibody wich presents a strong agonist effect through CD40 aggregation, due to its lack of flexibility in the hinge region, and limited FcγR-binding to prevent APCs depletion.

Camrelizumab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, camrelizumab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Camrelizumab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Mode of action (MOA): tacatresgene autoleucel recognize and bind to tumor cells expressing the targeted neoantigen, resulting in a cytotoxic T-lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated immune response against the patient's tumor cells. Mutated cancer-specific proteins displayed on a patient cancer cell surface (or 'neoantigens') contain 'neoepitopes' (neoE), which are specifically recognized by T cells within the patient's own immune response. The imPACT Isolation Technology® allows to identify and capture these rare neoE-specific CD8+ T cells from peripheral blood. Polyclonal autologous neoE TR sequences of these cells are extracted and engineered, and by CRISPR/Cas9 technology, used to replace natural TR in autologous fresh CD8+ and CD4+ T cells for re-injection in the patient.

Tremelimumab targets and binds to cytotoxic T-lymphocyte-associated antigen 4 (CTLA4), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on T cells that downregulates both proliferation and activation of T cells. Once bound to CTLA4, tremelimumab blocks its binding to its ligand CD80/CD86 molecules and promotes CD80/CD86 binding to CD28 co-stimulatory receptor. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by CTLA4 signaling and promotes stimulatory CD28 pathway resulting in expansion and activation of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Tremelimumab is an IgG2-Kappa antibody with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Hybrid: A hybrid antibody is one in which one-half of the antibody (one heavy chain and one light chain) is from one species origin whereas the other half is from another species origin.
An hybrid antibody is identified by a pre-stem made of each species pre-stem separated by the letter 'x' in the INN. For example, a rat-murine hybrid antibody is identified by the pre-stem -axo-. catumaxomab was generated by the fusion of a mouse B cell, a rat B cell and a myeloma cell to form a quadroma cell line (Triomab® technology) (Zeidler R. et al. 1999, PMID: 10415020). It is the first approved bispecific antibody (in 2009 in EU).

Mezagitamab targets and binds to CD38 molecule, a type II transmembrane glycoprotein expressed in immune cells, including natural killer cells, monocytes, and activated T cells and plasma cells. Durvalumab targets and binds to programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274,PD-L1), an immunosuppressive ligand expressed on tumor cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to CD274, durvalumab blocks its binding to its receptor programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1) expressed on activated T cells, leaving the PDCD1 pathway intact through its second ligand, PDCD1LG2 (PD-L2), which is less expressed in tumor cells, to maintain inhibitory signals mediated by PDCD1LG2/PDCD1. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Durvalumab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with three mutations in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v39 CH2 F1.3, E1.2, S116) to reduce FcγR binding and antibody-dependent cytoxicity (ADCC) or complement-dependent cytoxicity (CDC), preventing T cells depletion.

Fianlimab targets and binds to lymphocyte activation gene 3 (LAG3, CD223), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to LAG3, fianlimab blocks its binding to its ligands expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APC). This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by the LAG3 signaling resulting in activation and proliferation of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response. Fianlimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding. In addition, fianlimab has three mutations in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G4v50 CH2 P1.4, V1.3, A1.2, delG1.1) to reduce any residual FcγR binding and antibody-dependent cytoxicity (ADCC) or complement-dependent cytoxicity (CDC), preventing T cells depletion.

Atezolizumab targets and binds to programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274,PD-L1), an immunosuppressive ligand expressed on tumor cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to CD274, atezolizumab blocks its binding to its receptor programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1) expressed on activated T cells, leaving the PDCD1 pathway intact through its second ligand, PDCD1LG2 (PD-L2), which is less expressed in tumor cells, to maintain inhibitory signals mediated by PDCD1LG2/PDCD1. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Atezolizumab is an IgG1-kappa aglycosylated antibody with one mutation in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v29 CH2 A84.4) resulting in loss of FcyR binding ability, to prevent T cells depletion.

Feladilimab targets and binds to inducible T cell co-stimulator (ICOS), a stimulatory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells. Once bound to ICOS, feladilimab stimulates ICOS signaling. This activation results in activation and proliferation of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Feladilimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding. In addition, feladilimab has one mutation in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G4v3 CH2 E1.2) to reduce antibody-dependent cytoxicity (ADCC) or complement-dependent cytoxicity (CDC), preventing T cells depletion.

Ezabenlimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, ezabenlimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Ezabenlimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Miptenalimab targets and binds to lymphocyte activation gene 3 (LAG3, CD223), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to LAG3, miptenalimab blocks its binding to its ligands expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APC). This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by the LAG3 signaling in resulting activation and proliferation of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response. Miptenalimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Envafolimab targets and binds to programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274,PD-L1), an immunosuppressive ligand expressed on tumor cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to CD274, envafolimab blocks its binding to its receptor programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1) expressed on activated T cells, leaving the PDCD1 pathway intact through its second ligand, PDCD1LG2 (PD-L2), which is less expressed in tumor cells, to maintain inhibitory signals mediated by PDCD1LG2/PDCD1. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Envafolimab is a single VH domain fused to an IgG1-Fc antibody with one mutation in the hinge region (IMGT variant G1v37 h S5) and two mutations in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v71 CH2 A27, G116) to reduce FcγR binding and antibody-dependent cytoxicity (ADCC) or complement-dependent cytoxicity (CDC), preventing T cells depletion.

Pucotenlimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, pucotenlimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Pucotenlimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion. In addition, pucotenlimab has mutations in the CH2 and CH3 domains (IMGT variant G4v22 CH2 T16, P91, CH3 A114) to increase FCGRT (FcRn) binding and antibody half-life by mAb recycling.

Dostarlimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, dostarlimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) expressed on tumor cells, and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2), primarily expressed on antigen-presenting cells (APCs). Dostarlimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Ipilimumab targets and binds to cytotoxic T-lymphocyte-associated antigen 4 (CTLA4), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on T cells that downregulates both proliferation and activation of T cells. Once bound to CTLA4, ipilimumab blocks its binding to its ligand CD80/CD86 molecules and promotes CD80/CD86 binding to CD28 co-stimulatory receptor. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by CTLA4 signaling and promotes stimulatory CD28 pathway resulting in expansion and activation of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Ipilimumab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and induce antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity (ADCC) for Treg cells depletion.

Cosibelimab targets and binds to programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274,PD-L1), an immunosuppressive ligand expressed on tumor cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to CD274, cosibelimab blocks its binding to its receptor programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1) expressed on activated T cells, leaving the PDCD1 pathway intact through its second ligand, PDCD1LG2 (PD-L2), which is less expressed in tumor cells, to maintain inhibitory signals mediated by PDCD1LG2/PDCD1. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Cosibelimab is an IgG1-lambda antibody with ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and induce antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity (ADCC) for potential enhanced efficacy in certain tumor types.

Pradusinstobart targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, pradusinstobart blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. Adebrelimab targets and binds to programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274,PD-L1), an immunosuppressive ligand expressed on tumor cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to CD274, adebrelimab blocks its binding to its receptor programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1) expressed on activated T cells, leaving the PDCD1 pathway intact through its second ligand, PDCD1LG2 (PD-L2), which is less expressed in tumor cells, to maintain inhibitory signals mediated by PDCD1LG2/PDCD1. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Adebrelimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding. In addition, adebrelimab has two mutations in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G4v4 CH2 CH2 A1.3, A1.2) to reduce any residual FcγR binding and antibody-dependent cytoxicity (ADCC) or complement-dependent cytoxicity (CDC), preventing T cells depletion.
IMGT proposed MOA on 29/11/2022.

Tislelizumab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, tislelizumab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) expressed on tumor cells, and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2), primarily expressed on antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Tislelizumab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding. In addition, tislelizumab has five mutations in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G4v37 CH2 P1.4, V1.3, A1.2, A27, V92) to reduce FcγR binding and antibody-dependent cytoxicity (ADCC) or complement-dependent cytoxicity (CDC), preventing T cells depletion by any residual ADCC activity; and one mutation in the CH3 domain (IMGT variant G4v6CH3 K88) to confer more stability.

Clazakizumab targets and binds to interleukin 6 (IL6), a cytokine responsible for participating in the processes of inflammation and the maturation of B cells. Once bound to IL6, clazakizumab effectively obstructs IL6-IL6R signaling, encompassing both the classic and trans IL6 signaling pathways through the Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitors. This interference leads to the prevention of both anti-inflammatory (phosphoinositide-3-kinase (PI3K) and protein kinase B / PKB pathway) and pro-inflammatory responses (signal transducer and activator of transcription 3-STAT3 and MAPK (mitogen-activated protein kinase) pathway). Clazakizumab is an IgG1-kappa aglycosylated antibody with one mutation in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v29 CH2 A84.4) resulting in loss of FcyR binding ability.

An antibody or immunoglobulin monomer is formed by two identical light chains and two identical heavy chains. There are five classes of immunoglobulins in human, IgM, IgD, IgG, IgA and IgE, each with distinct heavy chains (mu, delta, gamma, alpha and epsilon, respectively).
When expressed at the surface of the B cells, immunoglobulins are anchored by their heavy chains. These membrane immunoglobulins (mIgM, mIgD, mIgG, mIgA and mIgE) are associated with CD79A (Ig-alpha, mb-1) and CD79B (Igbeta, B29). One CD79A-CD79B heterodimer and one immunoglobulin monomer constitute the B cell receptor (BcR).
When secreted by the plasmocytes, immunoglobulins are monomeric (IgG, IgD, IgE, and IgA in serum), dimeric (IgA in seromucous secretions), or pentameric (IgM).

Lodapolimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, lodapolimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Lodapolimab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with five mutations in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v43-60 CH2 A1.3, E1.2, A1, S115, S116) to reduce FcγR binding and antibody-dependent cytoxicity (ADCC) or complement-dependent cytoxicity (CDC), preventing T cells depletion.

Botensilimab targets and binds to cytotoxic T-lymphocyte-associated antigen 4 (CTLA4), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on T cells that downregulates both proliferation and activation of T cells. Once bound to CTLA4, botensilimab blocks its binding to its ligand CD80/CD86 molecules and promotes CD80/CD86 binding to CD28 co-stimulatory receptor. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by CTLA4 signaling and promotes stimulatory CD28 pathway resulting in expansion and activation of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Botensilimab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with three mutations in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v8 CH2 D3, L115, E117) to enhance antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytoxicity (ADCC) for Treg cells depletion.

Tagraxofusp comprises the catalytic (C) and transmembrane (T) domains of a truncated diphtheria toxin (DT) (does not contain the receptor binding domain) fused to the full length Homo sapiens IL3. 524 aa: (1-389) DT – (390-391) His-Met – (392-424) IL-3 MoA. By the C-terminal IL3, tagraxofusp engages tumor cells over-expressing interleukin-3 receptor subunit alpha (CD123), such as blastic plasmacytoid dendritic-cell neoplasm (BPDCN) and is internalized. The hematological cancer cells are killed by the cytotoxic truncated diphtheria toxin (DT).

IMGT-ONTOLOGY manages the immunogenetics knowledge through diverse facets that rely on the seven axioms of the Formal IMGT-ONTOLOGY, or IMGT-Kaleidoscope: "IDENTIFICATION", "CLASSIFICATION", "DESCRIPTION", "NUMEROTATION", "LOCALIZATION", "ORIENTATION" and "OBTENTION". These axioms postulate that any object, any process and any relation have to be identified, classified, described, numbered, localized and orientated, and that the way it is obtained can be characterized. As the same axioms can be used to generate concepts for multi-scale level approaches, the Formal IMGT-ONTOLOGY represents a paradigm for system biology ontologies, which need to identify, to classify, to describe, to number, to localize and to orientate objects, processes and relations at the molecule, cell, tissue, organ, organism or population levels.

Tafasitamab targets and binds to CD19 antigen, a member of the immunoglobulin superfamily (IgSF). CD19 is continuously and stably expressed on all stages of B lineage differentiation on the surface of B cell lymphocytes. Once bound to CD19, tafasitamab depletes circulating CD19+ B cells, thus suppressing inflammatory responses and impairing B cell dependent T cell activation. Tafasitamab is an IgG1-IgG2-kappa hybrid antibody (G1G2v1) with increased ability to bind to FcyRIII through mutations in the CH2 domain (G2v16 CH2 D3, A110, E117) to induce antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC) and antibody-dependent cellular phagocytosis (ADCP) for enhanced efficacy against plasma cells and auto-antibodies production.

(ONTAK®) is a recombinant fusion protein consisting of peptide sequences for the enzymatically active and membrane translocation domain of diphtheria toxin linked to human IL-2. Denileukin diftitox specifically binds to IL2 receptors on the cell membrane, is internalized via receptor-mediated endocytosis and inhibits protein synthesis by ADP ribosylation of elongation factor 2, resulting in cell death.
In 2001, Ligand Pharmaceuticals Inc, Elan submitted denileukin diftitox for marketing in Europe as ONZAR, for treatment of lymphoma cutaneous T cell (CTCL).

Rituximab targets and binds to MS4A1 (membrane-spanning 4-domains subfamily A member 1, CD20), a B cell surface molecule with a role in the development and differentiation of B cells into plasma cells. Once bound to MS4A1, rituximab depletes circulating CD20+ B cells by apoptosis. Rituximab is an IgG1-kappa antibody able to induce complement-dependent cytotoxicity (CDC), antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC) and antibody-dependent cellular phagocytosis (ADCP) to indirectly deplete CD20+ expressing cells.
